metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students engage in ensemble theatre production, developing performance skills and stage presence within a company context. This course emphasizes collaboration, rehearsal discipline, and the shared responsibility of creating compelling theatre experiences. Students learn the professionalism and artistic commitment required in theatre ensemble work while exploring diverse performance styles and theatrical traditions.
metadata.general_description: Develop as a theatre performer within an ensemble context, learning performance discipline and collaborative production.
cross_curricular_connections: English Language Arts (script analysis and interpretation)
History and Social Studies (cultural contexts and social themes)

big_ideas.statement: Theatre is a collaborative art form where individual performers contribute to collective artistic creation.
big_ideas.elaboration: Each ensemble member's focus, commitment, and artistic choices directly impact the overall quality and meaning of theatre productions; performers must balance individual character creation with ensemble cohesion.

big_ideas.statement: Disciplined rehearsal processes develop character understanding and build ensemble ensemble trust.
big_ideas.elaboration: Through structured rehearsal methodologies, performers learn to interpret scripts, make character choices, collaborate with scene partners, and develop the depth necessary for compelling performance.

big_ideas.statement: Stage presence and focused performance in front of audiences requires preparation, technique, and authenticity.
big_ideas.elaboration: Theatre performers develop their ability to execute scenes, maintain character focus, connect with scene partners and audience, and respond to the live performance environment.

big_ideas.statement: Theatre companies preserve and transmit theatrical traditions while creating contemporary artistic expression.
big_ideas.elaboration: Through study of classic and contemporary theatre, ensemble members develop understanding of theatrical heritage and participate in the ongoing evolution of theatrical art.

competencies.raw: Develop and embody theatrical characters with depth and authenticity
i_can_statements: I can analyze scripts to identify character motivations, relationships, and arc of development
I can make deliberate character choices about physicality, voice, and emotional expression
I can develop character continuity and consistency throughout rehearsal and performance
I can explore character relationships and dynamics with scene partners

competencies.raw: Create compelling scenes and theatrical moments through focused ensemble work
i_can_statements: I can perform scenes with attention to subtext, objectives, and emotional truth
I can maintain character focus and believability within theatrical scenarios
I can build effective stage relationships and react authentically to scene partners
I can adjust performance choices based on feedback and rehearsal discovery

competencies.raw: Explore diverse performance styles and theatrical traditions
i_can_statements: I can adapt performance style and technique to different theatrical genres and periods
I can explore performance approaches from various theatrical traditions and cultures
I can experiment with movement, voice, and physical expression across styles
I can develop versatility as a performer across different theatrical contexts

competencies.raw: Contribute creative energy and ideas to collaborative theatre production
i_can_statements: I can bring focus, commitment, and positive energy to rehearsals and performances
I can share ideas and observations that contribute to scene and production development
I can collaborate with other performers to solve artistic and technical challenges
I can support and encourage fellow ensemble members in their artistic development

competencies.raw: Analyze scripts and understand dramatic structure and thematic content
i_can_statements: I can identify plot structure, character arcs, and thematic elements in scripts
I can analyze dialogue for subtext, tone, and emotional content
I can understand the historical or cultural context of scripts and theatrical works
I can articulate how playwright choices create meaning and theatrical impact

competencies.raw: Evaluate my own performance and growth as an ensemble theatre artist
i_can_statements: I can assess my character development and performance choices with honesty
I can recognize strengths and areas for growth in my ensemble work
I can reflect on my commitment to rehearsal discipline and company standards
I can set goals for continued development as a theatre performer

competencies.raw: Understand the role of individual performers within collective theatrical vision
i_can_statements: I can explain how my performance choices affect ensemble dynamics and audience experience
I can recognize the interdependence of performers in creating coherent theatrical works
I can reflect on the balance between character individuality and ensemble unity
I can appreciate the contribution of each company member to production success

competencies.raw: Develop professional standards and artistic discipline in theatre company membership
i_can_statements: I can commit to rehearsal attendance, punctuality, and focused participation
I can respond constructively to direction from directors and fellow ensemble members
I can maintain professional conduct in company contexts and performances
I can demonstrate the discipline and professionalism expected of theatre artists

competencies.raw: Perform characters and scenes with technical skill and authentic presence
i_can_statements: I can execute character choices and scenes with clarity, commitment, and presence
I can maintain focus and emotional authenticity throughout performance
I can express character depth and relationships through performance
I can respond to live audience energy and performance conditions

competencies.raw: Collaborate and communicate effectively in rehearsal and performance contexts
i_can_statements: I can communicate clearly with directors, ensemble members, and support staff
I can listen and respond to feedback and direction with openness
I can coordinate with other performers to maintain scene integrity and ensemble cohesion
I can support the theatrical experience of fellow company members

competencies.raw: Document and reflect on theatrical learning and company experiences
i_can_statements: I can record performances and analyze my artistic choices and growth
I can maintain rehearsal notes and character documentation
I can write reflectively about theatrical learning and ensemble processes
I can articulate insights gained through theatre company participation

competencies.raw: Engage audiences through authentic theatrical storytelling and presence
i_can_statements: I can connect with audiences through genuine character performance and focus
I can communicate the emotional truth and themes of theatrical work to audiences
I can present myself professionally in performance contexts
I can speak about my theatrical experience and the significance of theatre ensemble work

competencies.raw: Connect theatre company work to broader cultural and historical contexts
i_can_statements: I can explore the historical and cultural significance of theatrical works and traditions
I can understand how theatre reflects and responds to social issues and contemporary concerns
I can make connections between theatre and other art forms or disciplines
I can recognize the role of theatre companies in contemporary culture

competencies.raw: Study diverse theatrical traditions and learn from theatre artists
i_can_statements: I can explore theatrical styles and traditions from multiple cultures and time periods
I can study the work and approaches of significant theatre directors and artists
I can recognize how different theatrical approaches shape meaning and audience experience
I can develop appreciation for diversity in contemporary and historical theatre

competencies.raw: Develop awareness of theatre careers and professional pathways
i_can_statements: I can explore career opportunities in theatre performance and ensemble contexts
I can understand the training, preparation, and commitment required for professional theatre
I can develop a performance portfolio that demonstrates my capabilities as a theatre artist
I can engage with mentorship and guidance from theatre professionals

competencies.raw: Contribute to building a supportive and inclusive theatre company culture
i_can_statements: I can model respect, support, and encouragement for fellow ensemble members
I can contribute to creating an inclusive environment where all performers feel valued
I can mentor younger or newer members of the theatre company
I can participate in building a community committed to theatrical excellence and mutual support

areas.name: Character Development and Scene Work
topics: Script analysis and character motivation identification
Character background development and psychological understanding
Physical and vocal character choices and embodiment
Scene partner relationships and effective stage communication
Building authenticity, subtext, and emotional truth in performance

areas.name: Rehearsal Processes and Ensemble Collaboration
topics: Director-actor collaboration and interpretation processes
Active listening and responsive ensemble work
Problem-solving approaches for artistic and technical challenges
Collaborative creativity and shared artistic decision-making
Building ensemble trust, cohesion, and mutual support
Professional standards, discipline, and company culture

areas.name: Performance Skills and Stage Presence
topics: Focus, energy, and presence in live performance contexts
Technical execution of blocking, movement, and staging
Vocal projection, articulation, and expression
Emotional authenticity and commitment in character performance
Adaptation to performance conditions and audience response
Professional preparation and management of performance challenges

areas.name: Theatrical Traditions, Styles, and Artistic Vision
areas.raw: Theatrical Traditions, Styles, and Artistic Vision
topics: Classical theatre traditions and performance approaches
Contemporary and experimental theatre styles and methodologies
Cultural theatre traditions and diverse performance practices
Script interpretation and directorial vision in theatrical context
The role of theatre companies in cultural expression and social commentary
Career pathways and opportunities in professional theatre
